title: Portuguese Lamb Paella
description: A traditional Portuguese dish featuring lamb, saffron, and spices, cooked in a tandoor-style pan for a silky and umami flavor.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, boneless and cut into 1-inch cubes
Tandoor-style pan or large paella pan
Saffron threads, soaked in 2 tablespoons hot water

instructions: Preheat the tandoor-style pan or large paella pan to 425°F (220°C).
Heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the lamb and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium and add the onion. Cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the garlic, oregano, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, cumin, and salt. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the browned lamb back into the pan, along with the keto-friendly Spanish rice, chicken broth, and red wine (if using). Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, covered, for 15-20 minutes or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is cooked.
Stir in the soaked saffron and its liquid. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Remove from heat and let rest, covered, for 5 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
